name: Mushroom Soup
recipeIngredient: 1 cup white part of leek only, chopped
salt and white pepper to taste
recipeInstructions:
1. Melt 4 tablespoons butter in a large skillet over medium heat.
2. Add the chopped shiitake, crimini, and button mushrooms; sauté for about 5 minutes until they begin to brown.
3. Stir in the leek white part, kale, and cooked wild rice. Continue sautéing until the vegetables are tender, about 2-3 minutes.
4. Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional minute until fragrant.
5. Sprinkle the flour over the mixture, stirring constantly to form a roux. Cook for about 3 minutes, ensuring the flour is fully incorporated and no raw taste remains.
6. Gradually add the chicken broth and half-and-half, stirring continuously to prevent lumps and achieve a smooth, creamy texture.
7. Simmer the soup on low heat until it thickens, approximately 10-15 minutes.
8. Season with salt and white pepper to taste.
9. Stir in the chopped chives and parsley just before serving for added freshness.
10. For an enhanced richness, add another tablespoon of butter at the end.
